ety: figār, q.v. + ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Wounding; afflicting, affliction (used as last member of compounds, e.g. dil-figārī).
(fī, q.v.+pron. hi), In him; in it; in that.

ety: perf. part. of fikandan, and = afganda; see fikandanī
def: part. Thrown or cast down; hung down (as the head); thrown away.

ety: fr. fikandan = afkandan or afgandan; rt. Zend kan with prep. aiwi; S. अभि+खन्
def: adj. Fit to be thrown (or cast, or spread, &c.); fit to be thrown away.
(inf. n. of فقد 'to lose,' &c.), s.m. & f. Loss, miss, deprivation, want.

ety: inf. n. of فقه 'to possess knowledge' (esp. of the law
def: s.f. Knowledge, understanding (esp. of the law); — the science of the (Muhammadan) law, jurisprudence; theology.

r: fikr, fikr karnā, kī, kā, fikr-mand
def: adj. Thoughtful; anxious; pensive, sorrowful
subs: (-kī), To be constantly thinking (of); to harbour designs (against); to be anxious to injure

adj. & s.m. Without thought, thoughtless; without forethought; without concern or anxiety, unconcerned; free from care; — a thoughtless or heedless person; — be-fikrī, s.f. Thoughtlessness; regardlessness, unconcern; freedom from care.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فِکْر (fikr), from Arabic فِكْر (fikr). First attested as Old Hindi फिकरि (phikari).

ety: for A. فقرة fiqrat, v.n. fr. فقر; see faqr
def: s.m. Vertebræ (of the back); — a choice phrase or sentence (as being likened to a vertebra of the back); fine or specious speech or words; — a line; a sentence, a period
subs: vulg. fiqre-bāz, adj. & s.m. Eloquent, glib-tongued, plausible, artful; — an artful fellow, a knave
To make up a fine sentence or speech; to make up or invent a story
s.f. Arrangement or construction of a sentence, composition; eloquence
or fiqra denā, To bring forward, or to present, fine or specious sentences or words, to wheedle or deceive with fine phrases; to deceive, cheat
(kisī-ke), To be deceived or taken in by the specious sentences or speeches (of); to be talked over (by).

def: adj. Of thought, thoughtful; anxious; pensive; sorrowful.
s.m. An elephant
subs: s.m. Elephant keeper; elephant-driver (syn. mahāwat, mahaut)
or fīl-pāʼoṅ, adj. & s.m. Having a swelling in the leg, having elephantiasis; — elephantiasis
(or pīl-pāya, vulg. pil pāya), s.m. lit. 'Elephant's leg'; a pillar or column; a post
adj. & s.m. Seated, or riding, on an elephant

ety: the arabicized form of Pers. pilpil = S. पिप्पलि pippali
def: s.f. Long pepper, pepper (syn. H. pīpal).
adj. Of or belonging to an elephant.
s.m. White copper; metal, ore; dross, scoria.
s.m. pl. (of filizz), The metals (viz. gold, silver, copper, iron, lead, tin, and tutenag); ores; mines.

ety: P. pingān arabacized; so also A. finjāl = P. pingāl, 'a cup'
def: s.m. A small porcelain (or earthenware) cup (out of which coffee, &c. is drunk).

ety: inf. n. of فاروق fāraqa, 'he separated' (himself from
def: s.m. Separation, disunion, abandonment; departing, departure (from); absence; distance, distinction; — anxiety, grief, regret (on account of absence)
subs: adj. & s.m. Afflicted by separation or disunion; separated; absent; — one so afflicted, one doomed to separation.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فِرَاق (firāq), borrowed from Arabic فِرَاق (firāq), from فَارَقَ (fāraqa). First attested in c. 1421 as Middle Hindi فراق (fraq /⁠firāq⁠/).

def: adj. & s.m. Of or relating to separation; separated (from a loved one); absent; — one who is separated (from a friend, &c.).
adj. Affronted, displeased, angry; rebellious, in a state of mutiny.
s.m. Flight, running away, absconding
subs: v.n. To flee, take to flight, run away, abscond.

def: adj. & s.m. Absconded; fugitive, runaway, escaped; — a person who has absconded, a fugitive, a runaway
subs: A fugitive, runaway, deserter; a cultivator who has absconded from or thrown up his land.

ety: for A. فراسة, v.n. fr. tafarrus, 'perceiving a thing intuitively,' &c., inf. n. v of فرس 'to be skilled' (in
def: s.f. Perception or discernment of (one's) internal or intrinsic condition or character, &c. by outward indications; physiognomy; intuitive perception, insight, discernment, sagacity, penetration, acuteness, understanding, judgment.

ety: inf. n. of فرش 'to spread' (a carpet
def: s.m. Spreading; strewing, &c.; — a thing that is spread (a bed, or carpet, &c.)
subs: s.m. A room in which carpets, &c. are kept.

def: s.m. Pharaoh (king of Egypt); — anyone inordinately proud, or insolent, or unbelieving; an audacious and cruel tyrant.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فِرْعَوْن (fir'awn), borrowed from Arabic فِرْعَوْن (firʕawn), possibly borrowed from Classical Syriac ܦܹܪܥܘܼܢ, borrowed from Koine Greek Φαραών (Pharaṓn), borrowed from Biblical Hebrew פַּרְעֹה, borrowed from Egyptian pr-ꜥꜣ, from pr + ꜥꜣ.

ety: firʻaun+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Pride, haughtiness, insolence, arrogance, audaciousness; tyranny.

ety: A. firdaus, prob. fr. the Pers.; Armen. partez; Heb. pardês; Gr. παράδεισος; Zend pairidaêza (pairi = S. परि; daêza, fr. rt. diz = S. दिह्)
def: s.m. A garden; Paradise
subs: adj. Located in paradise, whose dwelling is in paradise, deceased (applied to an emperor or great personage).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فردوس (firdaws), from Arabic فِرْدَوْس (firdaws), ultimately from Proto-Iranian *paridayjah.

def: s.m. Name of a famous Persian poet, the author of the great epic poem Shāh-nāma (he lived about A.D. 1000).

ety: rt. of firebīdan, or fireftan = fariftan = fardaftan; rt. Zend dab with prep. fra = S. प्र + दभ् (दम्भ्, दभनोति)
def: part. adj. Deceiving, cheating; alluring, seducing, captivating, winning (used as last member of compounds, e.g. mardum-fareb, 'deceiving or betraying men'; — dil-fireb, 'heart-alluring'); — s.m. Deception, deceit, fraud, trick, duplicity, treachery, imposture, delusion, fallacy; allurement, beguilement, &c.
subs: adj. & s.m. Deceitful, cheating; alluring; — a cheat, &c.
part. adj. Deceived, cheated, duped, gulled
s.f. Deceiving, deceit, imposing upon, cheating, swindling
(-ko), To deceive, impose upon, cheat, delude, defraud, dupe, gull; to allure, beguile
adj. & s.m. Practising deceit or treachery, deceitful, fraudulent, treacherous; — an impostor, a cheat
s.f. The practice of deceit or treachery, deceitfulness, deceit, imposture, &c.
(-se, or -par), To be deceived or deluded (by), be taken in (by), be duped, be gulled, be cheated (by), be misled (by)

ety: imperf. part. of fireftan; see fireb
def: part. adj. & s.m. Deceiving; — deceived; — a deceiver.

ety: perf. part. of fireftan; see fireb
def: part. Deceived, deluded; seduced; infatuated; fascinated, charmed, enamoured; — s.m. & f. Deluded person; doter, &c.
subs: v.t. To delude; to seduce; to fascinate, charm
To be deceived or deluded (by); to be fascinated (by), to be enamoured (of, -kā), be madly in love (with).

ety: fir = far = Zend fra ( = S. प्र)+ ishta = Zend aśta, aś = S. अश् (अश्नोति)
def: s.m. An angel; a messenger; a missionary; an apostle; a prophet
subs: or firishta-ṣifat, adj. Angel-tempered; angelic, heavenly; god-like, godly
ī denā (-ko), Angels to appear (to); to be on the point of death.

def: s.m. pl. (of firishta), Angels, &c.

ety: perf. part. of firistādan; and = istāda, q.v.+pref. fir = far = Zend fra = S. प्र
def: part. Sent; — s.m. One who is sent, a messenger; an ambassador, envoy, a legate.

ety: for A. فرقة firqat, v.n. fr. فرق; see farq
def: s.m. A distinct body or class (of men), a party, body, troop, company, society, class, sect, tribe, kind.
s.f. A dish resembling hasty pudding, made of ground rice, milk, and sugar (cf. A. furnī, 'thick bread,' &c.; rel. n. fr. furn, 'an oven').

ety: v.n. fr. firoḵẖtaṅ; Zend rt. vać with prep. fra = S. प्र+वच्
def: s.f. Selling; sale
part. (of firoḵẖtan; see firoḵẖt), Sold.
part. adj. & s.m. Selling; — seller (used as last member of compounds, e.g. mewa-farosh, 'fruit-seller'); — s.f. Selling, sale ( = faroḵẖt)

ety: firosh, q.v.+anda = Zend aff. anṭ = S. अन्त्
def: imperf. part. & n. of agency (of firoḵẖtan), Selling; — one who sells, seller, vendor

ety: firosh, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Selling (used in comp., e.g. mewa-faroshī, 'fruit-selling').

ety: for pīroz, peroz; Pehl. peroj; Zend pairi+raoćaṅh, rt. ruć; S. परि+रोचः, rt. रुच्
def: adj. Victorious, triumphant; successful, prosperous, fortunate, happy
subs: or fīroz-baḵẖt, or fīroz-mand, adj. Victorious, conquering

ety: for pīroza, peroza, rel. n. fr. fīroz, q.v.; — the S. पेरोज is prob. formed fr. the Pers.
def: s.m. A turquoise (com. regarded as a stone that brings good luck).

ety: fīroz, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Victory, triumph; success, prosperity, good fortune; — (rel. n. fr. fīroza), adj. Of the colour of the turquoise, azure, blue.
s.f. Fees.

ety: fr. fishāndan = afshāndan = shawāndan, caus. of shudan; rt. Zend shu with prep. aiwi = S. अभि+च्यु (च्यवते)
def: part. adj. & s.m. Scattering, strewing; shedding; spreading, expanding, diffusing; strewed, — scatterer, strewer, &c. (used as last member of compounds, e.g. ātash-fishān, 'scattering fire').

ety: perf. part. of fishāndan; see fishān
def: part. Scattered, strewed; shed, spilt; spread, diffused.

ety: fishān, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Scattering, strewing, &c. (used, like fishān, in comp., e.g. jān-fishānī, q.v.).

ety: inf. n. of فسق 'to quit the right way,' &c
def: s.m. Vicious or immoral conduct; sin, iniquity; impiety; falsehood; immorality; adultery; obscenity; impudence
subs: s.m. Sin and iniquity; vice and immorality; adultery, &c.

ety: fi = af = Zend aiwi = S. abhi+sos = soz? q.v.
def: s.m. &c. = afsos, q.v.
s.m. Ribbon; tape.
part. = uftāda, q.v.
s.f. = uftādagī, q.v.

ety: for فتنة fitnat, quasi-inf. n. of فتن 'to burn'; to try by fire,' &c.
def: s.m. Trial, affliction, calamity, mischief, evil, torment, plague, pest (applied to persons as well as things, e.g. yěh laṛkā bhī baṛā fitna haī); — temptation, seduction; — discord, conflict, cabal, faction, civil war, sedition, revolt, mutiny; perfidy; sin, crime
subs: v.n. To raise a revolt or sedition, &c.
or fitna-pardāz, adj. & s.m. Mischievous, seditious, turbulent, insurgent, &c.; — a mischief-maker, a fomenter of disturbances, a turbulent fellow, an incendiary, &c.
adj. & s.m. Seeking disturbance, seditious; quarrelsome, turbulent; — a quarrelsome or turbulent fellow, &c.
adj. & s.m. Mischief-making, sedition-causing; — mischief-maker, a seditious person, &c.
s.m. A species of mimosa or acacia bearing flowers and having a powerful scent.

ety: for A. فطنة, inf. n. of فطن
def: s.f. = fat̤ānat, q.v.

ety: v.n. fr. افطر aft̤ara, 'to break a fast,' iv of فطر; see fat̤r
def: s.m. The breaking of a fast; — the festival held on the breaking of the fast of Ramazān ( = īduʼl-fit̤r, q.v.s.v. ʻīd).
s.m. = fit̤rat, q.v.
s.m. Saddle-straps; cords fixed to a saddle for hanging game to.

ety: for A. فطرة, v.n. fr. فطر; see fat̤r
def: s.f. Creation; production; — the natural constitution, or nature, or form (with which one is created); — nature, constitution; natural disposition or temper; a natural or innate quality or property; idiosyncrasy; — wisdom, sagacity, shrewdness, skill, ingenuity; astuteness, cunning; deceit, trick, artifice, device, plan, scheme, plot, intrigue, machination, stratagem; — (fr. fit̤r, q.v.) the alms of the breaking of the fast of Ramazān
subs: v.n. To exercise (one's) skill or ingenuity; to practise artifices or stratagems; to plot and counterplot; to cheat, to shuffle.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فطرت (fitrat), from Arabic فِطْرَة (fiṭra, “human nature”).

def: adj. & s.m. Natural, constitutional; — sagacious, shrewd; cunning, artful, designing, crafty; — a cunning or artful person, &c.

ety: for afzā, rt. & imperat. of afzūdan; Pārsī awazā; rt. Zend śā with prep. aiwi = S. अभि+शा (शो, श्यति)
def: part. adj. Increasing, augmenting, enhancing (used as last member of compounds, e.g. jān-fazā, 'life-increasing').

ety: fizā, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Increasing, &c. (used, like fazā, in comp.).

ety: inf. n. of فحش 'to be gross, or lewd, or indecent,' &c.
def: s.m. Gross, foul, immodest, lewd, or obscene, speech or language; grossness, obscenity (in word or deed), ribaldry, bawdry; — adj. Gross, indecent, obscene, &c. (e.g. fǒḥsh bāteṅ, 'foul or indecent language;' fǒḥsh kitāb, 'an obscene book').
wety: From Arabic فحش (fuḥš).

def: s.m. = فوطه fot̤a, q.v.
s.m. A bag; purse; the scrotum, the testicles; — tax, revenue, collections
subs: (-meṅ), To pay a tax or revenue
vulg. fot̤e-dār, or fot̤-dār, s.m. Treasurer, banker, cashier, cash-keeper; a collector of tenant-dues
vulg. fot̤e-dārī, s.f. Bankership, treasurership; — the office of a collector of tenant-dues.

def: s.m. Valerian.
s.m. The heart.

ety: v.n. fr. فجل 'to be thick, or flaccid'
def: s.m. A radish, Raphanus sativus; — the heel; — adj. Thick.
s.m. Wickedness; immorality; adultery; lust.
s.m. pl. (of faqīh), Lawyers; theologians.
s.m. pl. (of faqīr), The poor; religious mendicants, derwishes.
s.f. Steel
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فولَاد (fōlād).

def: adj. Of steel, steel-; — s.f. A pike-staff.
adj. & s.m. Such a; such and such; a certain (man); — such a man, such a one; so and so; such a thing; — the unmentionable thing or part, penis virilis.

ety: for A. فلانة fulānat, fem. of fulān
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī) = fulān, q.v.; — falānī, s.f. Pudendum muliebre.
s.m. (pl. of fals, q.v.; but used as a sing. also), Small copper coins; — a copper coin, a paisa (Ang. 'pice'); money.

ety: an arabicised form; also bunduq, 'a ball, bullet'; 'a nut' (whence prob. bundūq or bandūq, 'a gun'); old forms are fendak, pendak; prob. akin to S. piṇṇa, or piṇḍaka, 'a ball'
def: s.f. A hazel-nut; — (met.) the stained tips of the fingers or toes (as being likened to hazel-nuts)
subs: s.f. Staining the tips of the fingers, &c. (to resemble the hazel-nut).

def: s.m. pl. (of fann, or fan), Ways, modes, varieties, diversities (rare); — arts, sciences; — arts, tricks, wiles, &c.
s.m. Name of a king of Kannauj (killed by Alexander the Great), Porus.

ety: for A. فرادَ furāda, quasi-plur. of fard, q.v.
def: adj. & adv. Sole, single, separate, solitary, unique; — singly, separately, one by one, one at a time, one after another
(v.n. fr. فرت 'to be sweet,' &c.), s.m. lit. 'Sweet, or thirst-subduing' (water); the river Euphrates.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فرات (furāt), from Arabic الْفُرَات (al-furāt), ultimately from Akkadian 𒀀𒇉𒌓𒄒𒉣 (^(ÍD)Purattu).

ety: inf. n. of فرق; see farq
def: s.m. (orig. 'Anything that makes a separation or distinction between truth and falsity'; (hence) the Qǒrʼān.

ety: for A. فرقة furqat, v.n. fr. فارق 'to separate oneself from,' &c., iii of فرق; see farq
def: s.f. Separation, disunion; abandonment; absence (of a beloved person); — distinction; distance ( = farq).

ety: for furoḵẖ, by change of خ to غ; see furoz, and furoḵẖta
def: s.m. Illumination, light, brightness, splendour; flame; — glory, fame, honour
subs: v.n. To get glory, or honour, &c.
v.n. To become famous, or illustrious, &c.

ety: of furoḵẖtan = afroḵẖtan; rt. Zend ruć with prep. aiwi or aibi = S. अभि+रुच् (रोचति)
def: Kindled, inflamed; burnt; — shining, radiant ( = afroḵẖta).

ety: rt. of furoḵẖtan = afroḵẖtan; see furoḵẖta
def: part. adj. Illumining, enlightening, kindling, inflaming (used as last member of compounds, e.g. dil-furoz, 'heart-inflaming'); — s.m. Splendour, brilliance, &c.
part. (of furoḵẖtan; see furoḵẖta, and furoz), Shining, luminous, resplendent; inflaming.

ety: for A. فرصة, v.n. fr. فرص 'to slit, cut, divide,' &c.
def: s.f. A time, opportunity, occasion; freedom (from), leisure; convenience; relief, recovery; respite, reprieve; rest, ease; (local) leave
subs: or furṣat milnā, v.n. To find or get time or leisure (for); to be at leisure; to find or get relief or respite (from, -se); to be dismissed or discharged (from); to obtain leave
(-ko), To grant or allow time or opportunity (for); to afford relief (to), to respite, reprieve; to give or grant leave (to)
adv. At (one's) leisure, or convenience, leisurely
(-ko), To have leisure, be at leisure; to be relieved, to recover (from illness).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فُرْصَت (fursat), borrowed from Arabic فُرْصَة (furṣa). First attested in c. 1503 as Middle Hindi فرصت (frṣt).

def: s.f. pl. (of فرع farʻ, q.v.), Tops, summits, heads; branches; subdivisions; ramifications; derivatives; — the derivative institutes of the law; fundamental principles or doctrines (of law or religion); — nobles, men of eminence (of a people or party), chiefs (of tribes or families).
s.f. pl. (of furūʻ), Summits; branches; ramifications ( = furūʻ, q.v.).
adj. Branch; collateral, indirect; additional; — s.f. Income or revenue other than that from a fixed source, additional income.
s.m. pl. (of farsh, q.v.), Carpets, floor-cloths, &c.
s.m. pl. (of firāsh), Carpets; beds.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فرش (farš), from Arabic فَرْش (farš, “carpet, rug”).

r: fushurda, fashurda, fushurdan, fashārdan, afshārdan, afshurdan, fashār
ety: perf. part. of fushurdan = fashārdan = afshārdan = afshurdan; see fashār
def: part. Squeezed, pressed, compressed; wrung
subs: adj. Having the heart wrung, afflicted.

ety: = afsūn, q.v. prob. akin to afsāna, q.v.
def: s.m. Enchantment, incantation, fascination, &c.
subs: adj. & s.m. Enchanting, fascinating, bewitching, &c.; — an enchanter, sorcerer, &c.

def: part. adj. = afsurda, q.v.
s.f. = afsurdagī, q.v.
s.m. A foot (of measure).
s.f. pl. (of fatḥ, q.v.), Victories, conquests; — gains, pickings, perquisites; income received gratuitously; a gratuitous supply (of sustenance, &c.); abundance, easy circumstances.
s.f. pl. (of futūḥ), Numerous victories or conquests.

ety: inf. n. of فتر 'to abate'; 'to become languid,' &c.
def: s.m. Languor, weakness, infirmity; defect, imperfection, unsoundness; irregularity; — coolness; discord, quarrelling, quarrel, row, riot
subs: or futūr ḍālnā, or futūr karnā (-meṅ), To create a disturbance, cause a quarrel, to sow discord (between)
s.m. Unsoundness of intellect or mind
s.m. Badness of digestion; indigestion, dyspepsia.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فتور (futūr), from Arabic فُتُور (futūr). Cognate with Bengali ফিতুর (phitur).

def: adj. & s.m. Dispersed; ruined, undone; — exciting quarrels; contentious, litigious; factious; riotous; — a fomenter of quarrels or disturbances, a contentious or litigious person, &c.
adj. & s.m. Dispersed; ruined, undone; — exciting quarrels; contentious, litigious; factious; riotous; — a fomenter of quarrels or disturbances, a contentious or litigious person, &c.
s.f. Generosity, honourableness, liberality, bountifulness.
s.m. pl. (of faẓīl = fāẓil, q.v.), The excellent; virtuous or good men; learned men, the learned.

ety: for A. فضالة fuẓālat, v.n. fr. فضل; see faẓl
def: s.m. A redundant portion; remaining portion, remains, remainder.
adj. = afzūd, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of فصل 'to exceed'; or pl. of faẓl, but used as a sing.
def: s.m. Excess, redundance, &c. ( = faẓl); violence; a busy, meddling spirit; impertinent interference; folly; — adj. Excessive, extravagant, exorbitant; redundant, superfluous, exuberant; unnecessary, needless, useless
subs: adj. & s.m. Extravagant, prodigal, lavish, profuse; — an extravagant person, a spendthrift
s.f. Excess of expenditure; extravagance, prodigality, lavishness, waste, dissipation
adj. & s.m. Talking much, talkative; prolix; — a prolix speaker; a great talker; a babbler; a boaster, an egotist.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فُضُول (fuzūl), borrowed from Arabic فُضُول (fuḍūl), from فَضَلَ (faḍala). First attested in c. 1649 as Middle Hindi فضول (fẓvl /⁠fuzūl⁠/).

ety: fuẓūl, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Excess, extravagance, exorbitance; superfluity, redundance, exuberance; — independence; — meddlesomeness, officiousness.
s.m. One who busies himself with that which does not concern him; one who acts without authority, or who is not a commissioned agent, &c.; a busy-body, meddler, an impertinent fellow.
adj. = afzūn, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Going, moving; — who or what goes (used as a suffix, e.g. kha-ga, vulg. khag, 'going in the air or sky; a bird,' &c.).
s.f. = gav, q.v.; — s.m. = garbh and garv, qq.v.

s.m. The tree Diospyros embryopteris, or D. glutinosa, or Embryopteris glutinifera (the viscid pulp of the ripe fruit is used as gum, in bookbinding; and in place of tar, for paying the seams of boats: the fruit contains much tannin; and the extract of the fruit is an excellent astringent); — pulp, pith, substance; — (dialec.) pregnancy ( = gābh, q.v.).
s.m. = gābhā, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of غبن 'to forget; to be deficient in judgment,' &c.
def: s.m. Forgetfulness; mistake; weakness of judgment or of mind; — adj. Weak in judgment, or in mind; liable to deception.
s.m. & adv. (colloq.), The sound made in gulping; — gulp-gulp, gobble-gobble.
s.m. = gavāśan, q.v.s.v. gava or gav.

ety: for A. غباوة, inf. n. of غبو 'to have little intelligence,' &c.
def: s.f. Weakness or deficiency of intellect; stupidity; inadvertency.
adj. Proud, haughty; rich, wealthy, prosperous.
s.m. (dialec.), A bedding.
adj. = gabbar, q.v.
adj. = gābhin, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Full, filled out, fleshy, plump, stout.
adj. (dialec.) = gabdā, q.v.
s.m. A dewlap; wattle (of a cock); a double chin
subs: s.m. A dimple in the chin ( = ćāh-ě-zanaḵẖ).

def: s.m. & adv. (colloq.), The sound made in gulping; — gulp-gulp, gobble-gobble.
s.m. Pregnancy (esp. of animals)
subs: To slip or cast a fœtus; to miscarry
The fœtus to be dropped; to miscarry.

def: s.m. A new leaf (springing from the centre of a plantain tree), a leaf in the bud; sprout, shoot; calyx (of a flower); unripe crop, half-ripe ear (of corn); inner portion (of wood), liber, alburnum, sap-wood; old cotton (taken out of a quilt, &c.); — fork (of a tree).

s.m. A new leaf (springing from the centre of a plantain tree), a leaf in the bud; sprout, shoot; calyx (of a flower); unripe crop, half-ripe ear (of corn); inner portion (of wood), liber, alburnum, sap-wood; old cotton (taken out of a quilt, &c.); — fork (of a tree).
s.f. A ray (of light), sunbeam, moon-beam.
s.m. The membrane in which the fœtus is enveloped (syn. kherī).
adj. Pregnant (esp. an animal); — s.f. A pregnant woman; an animal with young.
adj. = gambhīr, and gahirā, qq.v.
v.t. To snub, chide.
adj. & s.m. = gabrū, q.v.
s.m. (rustic) The hair of a new-born child.

ety: rel. n. fr. غبا, rt. غبو 'to lack intelligence'
def: adj. Having little or no intelligence, weak in mind; ignorant; stupid, dull; — unmindful, forgetful, neglectful, heedless, inadvertent.
adj. = gābhin, q.v.

ety: act. part. of غبر 'to remain, last,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Remaining, lasting, continuing; the later; the relic; the remainder; — passing, passing away; past.

ety: inf. n. of غبن 'to cheat, deceive, defraud,' &c.
def: s.m. Cheating, imposing upon, defrauding, &c.; deceit, fraud, imposition; loss (of money, &c.); misappropriation
subs: or g̠abn-ě-fāsh, s.m. A gross or a notorious fraud

def: s.m. A follower of Zoroaster, a fire-worshipper; an infidel.

ety: for gabhrū = Prk. गब्भ+रूवो = S. गर्भ+रूपः
def: adj. & s.m. Childish, puerile, juvenile; — stout and comely; — a boy, lad, stripling, a beardless youth; an unformed youth, a clown, a lout; — a bridegroom
s.m. (dialec.) = gāćh, q.v.

ety: Pehl. gać; Zend vićićā; perhaps S. विचित्र, or विस्तृतं
def: s.m. Mortar; cement; shell-lime; plaster; old mortar knocked off walls, &c.; — a floor, &c. plastered with lime
subs: s.f. Plastering; working (or building) with mortar or cement.

def: s.m. The sound made in walking through mire; or in piercing one with a spear or sword, &c.
s.m. The sound of walking in mire; sonum congressus in coitu; the sound from striking with a sword or spear, &c.; — slapping; clapping
subs: or g̠ać-g̠ać, s.m. = g̠ać (with the idea of repetition); — and = g̠ap-ā-g̠ap, q.v.

def: adj. (dialec.), Thick together, crowded, &c. (com. used in the redupl. form)
subs: or gić-pić, adj. & adv. Close, thick; crowded or stuffed together; packed close; stuck close together; — confused, jumbled; — squashy; — muddy; — all together; in a mass; — s.m.(f.?), A dense crowd; a confused mass; a jumble (cf. kać-pać)
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Closely-packed; thick-set; squat; short and fat; — a thick-set man; a short and fat person.

def: s.m. = g̠ać, q.v.
s.m. = g̠ać, q.v.
s.m. A tree
s.f. A pad for a beast of burden.
adj. Made of mortar or cement cemented; plastered.

ety: by boys, for playing at tip-cat
def: s.m. The game of tip-cat.
s.m. Miriness, muddiness; mire; squash; jumble; — adj. Squashy; jumbled, higgledy-piggledy; indistinct, confused, thick (as speech, &c.).

ety: by boys, for playing at tip-cat
def: s.m. The game of tip-cat.
s.m. = گد gad, q.v.
s.f. Sediment, dirt, dregs, lees; the impure liquor from an indigo vat
subs: (-kī), To stir up the sediment or dirt (of), to make a puddle

def: s.m. (dialec.) = gaḍhā or gaṛhā, q.v.; — s.f. = gāṛh, q.v.
s.f. A thud; a bump
subs: or gad-ā-bad, or gad-bad, adv. One upon another, one after another, successively; helter-skelter, pell-mell; higgledy-piddledy, all of a heap; — all at once, at a stroke

def: s.m. Boundary mark or pillar.
s.m. = gāṛā, q.v.
s.f. A mace; club, bludgeon
subs: s.m. The mace-bearer, an epithet of Krishṇa
s.m. 'The hero of the club,' an epithet of Krishṇa
vulg. gadā-judh, s.m. A fight with clubs or bludgeons.
wety: From Sanskrit गुद (guda).

def: s.m. A beggar, mendicant; — adj. Poor, indi gent
subs: s.f. Begging, mendicity; living on charity.
wety: From Sanskrit गुद (guda).

def: s.m. Speaking, speech; a word; a sentence; — a spell; — disease, sickness; — name of the younger brother of Krishṇa.
s.m. corr. of gadhā, q.v.
s.m. Unripe wheat or barley, &c. parched in the ear (for food).

ety: gadā, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Begging, mendicity; — beggary, poverty
s.m. Plumpness, fatness and softness of body (of a youth or a girl).
s.m. (Dakh.) = gādur, or bādur, qq.v.
s.m. A crowbar.
s.m. (Dakh.) An elephant's saddle or pad.
s.m. Marshy soil; moist land which retains water.
s.f. (dialec.) A magazine, store-room, &c. ( = godām, q.v.).

ety: gād˚, rt. of gādnā+S. अलः or अरः
def: adj. Half-ripe (grain or fruit); — s.m. (dialec.) A hean, stack, rick.
s.f. = gāḍar, q.v.

ety: Prk. गड्डारिआ or गड्डारी; S. गान्धार + इका or ई; so called because originally brought from the country of Gāndhāra or Kandahar
def: s.f. A sheep, an ewe; — a variety of the khas-khas grass ( = gāṅdar).
(a rustic form of gaddā or gaddī), s.f.(?) The seat of the driver at a Persian wheel.
s.m. A shepherd (by caste or occupation), a goat-herd; a cowherd
subs: s.m. A shepherd's tale; — rustic lore
A song of shepherds; a pastoral.

r: gāḍarū, gāḍrū, gāḍaru, gāḍru, gāṛrū, gāṛru
def: s.m. A mantr or charm against poison or snake-bite; — a snake-charmer; a juggler.
adv. Like a beggar.
adj. & adv. = gaṛ-baṛ, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Plump, fat, corpulent (cf. gabdā).
adv. With gladness, joyously, gaily, cheerfully; freely, openly; certainly, assuredly.
v.n. To be inarticulate from joy or grief, &c.; to rejoice ( = gadgadānā, q.v.).
s.m. Prose.

ety: gād˚, rt. of gādnā, q.v.+Prk. अओ = S. अ+कः
def: s.m. A soft quilted bedding; a large cushion (cf. gaddī); — a sheaf or bundle of fodder (ćarī).
wety: From Sanskrit गुद (guda).

def: s.m. = gaṛhā, q.v.
s.m. = gaḍḍā, q.v.

s.m. A lump (of earth or clay, &c.), a clod; a small heap, a mound or bank of earth; a boundary pillar, boundary mark; an indurated gland, a glandular swelling; a knotty tumour; a kernel; — any tuberose root, a bulb.
s.m. = gaṛhā, q.v.

ety: S. गड्डारिका, Prk. form, see gāḍar
def: s.f. A single ewe going in front of a flock of sheep
subs: s.m. A continuous line of sheep; a multitude crowded together (or following a leader) like a ffock of sheep; the act of following the example of another (syn. bheṛiyā-dhasān).

ety: intens. n. fr. غدر 'to act perfidiously,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Very perfidious, faithless, treacherous; deceitful, fraudulent, sly; — a perfidious or faithless man, &c., a betrayer (the epithet is also applied to a large city, &c. as being a place in which one is easily lost, and a thing or person is hard to find).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غَدّار, from Arabic غَدَّار (ḡaddār, “traitor, swindler”). First attested in c. 1665 as Middle Hindi غدار (ġdar).

def: adj. Unripe, half-ripe, nearly ripe (fruit or corn); — filled out; plump, well-developed; thick.
s.f. = gaddharī, q.v.
s.f. The unripe pod of gram or chick-pea, Cicer arietinum.
s.f. A boundary pillar or mark.

s.f. A cushion, padded seat, pad; bedding, or anything stuffed; a pack-saddle; royal cushion, throne; seat of some eminent personage; a sheet, carpet, or mat (on which one sits); the sitting-carpet of a banker; — a bandage (for dressing a wound); a menstruous cloth; a pessary; — a bundle of ten quires (of paper); — a sheaf (of corn)
subs: (-ko), To seat on the royal cushion; to instal (a king)
To put a bandage (on, -par, or -meṅ); to apply a pessary (to, -meṅ), to use a pessary
(-ko), To remove from the royal cushion or throne, to dethrone
s.m. One who sits on a cushion; a prince regent; a president, &c.
(vulg. -nashīnī), s.f. The sitting on a cushion; accession to the throne.

ety: i.q. gaṭṭā or gaṭṭhā, and gaṭhrī
def: s.f. A bundle (of grass, &c.); a bunch (of greens, &c.); a bundle of paper (containing ten quires).

ety: prob. god, q.v.+Prk. इल्लो; S. इलः; but cf. gādar, and gadrā
def: s.m. A babe in arms; a child.
s.m. A thick bedding; a case stuffed with cotton (to sit upon), a cushion; an elephant's pad.
s.m. = gaḍariyā, q.v.
s.m. A mace, a club; a bat; a stick; a foil or blunt wooden sword (used in fencing).
s.f. The sound of walloping or rapid boiling (cf. khad-khad, and khadbad); swelter.

adj. Inarticulate (from joy or grief, &c.), speaking thickly or convulsively, faltering; rejoicing, joyful; — (dialec.) adv. Falteringly; convulsively, with violent grief or sobbing; — gladly, freely, &c. ( = gad-badā-ke, q.v.)
s.m. The seed or grain of Guilandina bonducella; — parched Indian corn, &c. (cf. gādā).

v.n. To swell with some vehement affection or emotion; to choke; to shake or tremble violently; to sob or weep convulsively; — to emit the sound gadgad (an article under ebullition); to get into a state of ebullition ( = khadkhadānā); — to swelter.
s.m. Ground full of holes and hollows, uneven ground; — a soil abounding in moisture.
adj. (Dakh.) = gadlā, q.v.

ety: S. कर्द+घूर्णः; Prk. घोलो; H. ghol
def: adj. & s.m. (lit. 'having the gād (q.v.) stirred up'), Turbid, muddy, &c. ( = gadlā); — muddy or dirty water.
s.f. = gād, q.v.
s.m. = گڙهہ gaṛh, q.v.
s.m. An ass, a donkey; (met.) a stupid fellow, blockhead, fool; — an ass-load
subs: s.m. A stupid fellow, a fool
s.m. 'Ass's wedding,' symbolized by sunshine and rain (cf. the English 'fox's wedding')
s.m. A place where asses lie down and roll (it is regarded with superstitious dread)
(-ko), 'To mount (one) on an ass,' to punish by public exposure of a criminal seated on an ass with his face blackened and turned towards the tail; (fig.) to disgrace
(-par), A plough drawn by asses to pass (over a demolished fort, or city, &c., by way of vengeance); to be razed, to be utterly demolished (a city, &c.)
(-par), To drive a plough drawn by asses (over a demolished fort, &c.); to demolish, to raze.

def: s.f. A she-ass (see gadhā).
s.f. = gaṛhī, q.v.
s.m. A hole, cavity, pit, &c. (see gaṛhā).

ety: gād˚, rt. of gādnā, q.v. + Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. A thin mattress; a cushion, &c. ( = gaddī); — (dialec.) a gabion.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Armed with a mace or club; — a mace-bearer; an epithet of Krishṇa.
s.f. corr. of gadhī, q.v.
s.f. A kind of grass (generally found growing with ḍūb, which it resembles, except in being about three times as large; it is much used as fodder).
adj. Unfaithful, treacherous, deceitful, not to be trusted; — s.f. A pool of water left by a torrent (and liable to fail); a place in which rain-water stagnates.
s.m. A mace, a club; a bat; a stick; a foil or blunt wooden sword (used in fencing).
adj. (f. -ī), Turbid, muddy, foul, dull, dirty (water, or other liquid)
subs: v.t. To make muddy or turbid, &c.; to stir up the sediment or dirt (in a pond, or a vessel, &c.); — to stir up the mud and mix it with the water in a rice-field preparatory to sowing the rice.

ety: prob. gaddā, q.v.+S. ल+कः; — but cf. daglā
def: s.m. A garment padded with cotton, a quilted garment.

ety: gadlā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं); — and āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+ इका
def: Turbidness, muddiness; dulness.

ety: gadlā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं); — and āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+ इका
def: Turbidness, muddiness; dulness.
adj. & adv. = gaḍ-baḍ = gaṛ-baṛ, q.v.

ety: gād˚, prob. for gādh˚ = S. गाध् 'to heap together'; or = S. गाह्
def: v.t. To press down, ram down; to stuff, cram (syn. dabānā; ṭhāsnā).

ety: Prk. गड्डअडओ, or गड्डवडओ; S. गर्तक+र+कः
def: s.m. = gaṛhā, and gaḍhillā, qq.v.

ety: inf. n. of غدر 'to act perfidiously,' &c.
def: s.m. Perfidy, faithlessness, ingratitude; fraud, villainy; mutiny, rebellion, sedition, riot, disturbance, confusion, tumult, noise, bustle
subs: or g̠adr maćānā, v.n. To create a disturbance, raise a riot, to mutiny, revolt, rebel, &c.

def: adj. Unripe, half-ripe, nearly ripe (fruit or corn); — filled out; plump, well-developed; thick.

r: gadrāhaṭ, gadrā, nā, āhaṭ, āwaṭ, gaṅṭhāwat
def: s.f. The state of being nearly ripe.
v.n. To be half-ripe (as fruit, &c.); to attain to puberty; to bloom; — to swell; to become inflamed (as the eye, &c.).
part. Become half-ripe, &c.
subs: part. adj. (f. gadrāʼī-huʼī), Half-ripened; nearly ripe, &c. ( = gadrā, q.v.).

def: s.m. A child's go-cart ( = gaṛoṛnā).
s.m. A flying-fox ( = bādur, q.v.).
s.m. Composition not metrical yet framed in accordance with harmony; harmonious or musical prose; prose (in general).

ety: Prk. गाई; S. गाविका; cf. gāʼo
def: s.f. A cow (as an epithet it is used in the sense of a 'poor, meek, gentle, quiet, or tractable person')
subs: s.m. Gall-stone, biliary concretion found in the gall-bladder of a cow or bullock

r: gāf, ga, kāf-ě-ʻajamī, kāf, g, go, ka-varg, abjad, ka, ga-kār
ety: it being unknown in the Arabic
def: s.m. The letter or the sound ga.
adj. Thick, stout, of close texture.

ety: for orig. گف gaf = گپ gap
def: adj. Thick, dense, stout, close, closely-woven, substantial.
s.m. = g̠aflat, q.v.

ety: intens. n. fr. غافر g̠āfir, rt. غفر 'to cover, veil; to forgive'
def: adj. & s.m. Covering and forgiving (sins), very forgiving (an epithet applied to God); — the Forgiver (of sins).

ety: act. part. of غفل 'to be unmindful or forgetful,' &c.; see g̠aflat
def: adv. Unmindful, forgetful, neglectful, negligent, heedless, inadvertent, inattentive, remiss, thoughtless, careless; indolent; imprudent; senseless, unconscious; — negligently, thoughtlessly, inadvertently, unconsciously, &c.; soundly (said of sleep)
subs: (-se), To be inattentive (to), to be negligent, or careless, &c. (in respect of)
(-se), To become forgetful, or thoughtless (of); to be or become negligent, &c. ( = g̠āfil rahnā).

ety: act. part. of غفر 'to cover, veil'; 'to pardon'
def: part. adj. & s.m. Covering, or forgiving (sins); — one who forgives or pardons.
adj. Covering, enclosing, or embracing the whole; great, large; many, numerous; all.

ety: for A. غفلة, inf. n. of غفل 'to become nnmindful or forgetful,' &c.
def: s.f. Unmindfulness, forgetfulness, neglectfulness, negligence, neglect, inattention, heedlessness, inadvertence, remissness, carelessness; — soundness (of sleep), unconsciousness, drowsiness, stupor, insensibility, a swoon
subs: adj. Inattentive, unmindful, negligent, careless, thoughtless
adj. Struck, or overcome, with carelessness, &c.
or ḵẖẉāb-ě-gaflat, Sound or unconscious sleep; sheer apathy or indifference.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غَفْلَت (ġaflat), from Arabic غَفْلَة (ḡafla).

def: adj. Thoughtless, careless, neglectful, negligent, &c.
s.m. Forgiveness, pardon, remission of sins, absolution
adj. = g̠af, q.v.

ety: v.n. fr. غفر 'to forgive'; see g̠affār
def: adj. & s.m. Forgiving, merciful, clement; — the Forgiving One.
s.m. The atmosphere, air; the sky, heavens, firmament, the regions or expanse above
subs: s.f. The dust of the ketkī or Pandanus odoratissimus
'The hen and chickens of the sky,' the Pleiades
v.n. To reach up to the sky, to soar high, to be sky-high.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit गगन (gagana).

def: s.f. = gāgrī, q.v.
= H گگرا गगरा gagarā, vulg. gagrā, s.m. = H گگري गगरी gagarī, vulg. gagrī, s.f. A water-pot, pitcher, jar.
= H گگري गगरी gagarī, vulg. gagrī, s.f. A water-pot, pitcher, jar.
s.f. A water-jar, pitcher; a guglet (cf. gagar, gagrā, gagrī).
= H گگرا गगरा gagarā, vulg. gagrā, s.m. A water-pot, pitcher, jar.
s.f. = gāgrī, q.v.
s.m. (f. -in), A subdivision of the Bhangī or sweeper caste; — a man of that caste.

ety: Pehl. gāś; old P. gāthu; Zend gātu; S. गातु, rt. गा
def: s.f. Place (used in comp.); — time; turn; — adv. Sometime
subs: or gāh-o-be-gāh, adv. In season and out of season, at all seasons, frequently; occasionally, now and then, rarely
or gāh-gāhe, adv. Some time or other, at times, sometimes, now and then; again and again, repeatedly, frequently
s.f. A place proper for hunting, hunting-ground
s.f. Place of worship; mosque; church; synagogue; temple

def: s.m. A rapacious aquatic animal; a crocodile; an alligator; a shark, &c.

ety: Prk. गही; S. ग्रहा; or rt. of gahnā, q.v.
def: s.f. A handle.
s.m. Old H. corr. of graha, and gr̤iha, qq.v.
s.f. contrac. of gāh, q.v.
s.f. corr. of gāthā, q.v.; — 2˚ = kathā, q.v.

ety: gahā˚ = Prk. गहावे˚ = S. ग्रह+आपि caus. aug.+āʼī = Prk.एअव्वअइआ = S. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका
def: s.f. The act of treading out corn by bullocks (see gāhnā).
s.m. (f. -ikā), Taker; seizer (e.g. jān-kā gāhak, 'one who takes or aims at life'); receiver, getter; accepter; approver, appreciator (e.g. guṇ-gāhak); — purchaser, buyer, chapman, vendor, dealer.
s.f. Joyous emotion; pleasure; exhilaration, the emotion of intoxication; — warmth, zeal, fervour, empressement.
s.f. Demand, vent, sale; transaction
s.m. A taker; a purchaser.
s.f. Taking; purchase.

ety: gahak˚ = Prk. गहक्के(इ), fr. S. ग्रहं+कृ
def: v.n. To be pleased or delighted; to be in a state of exhilaration, to be agitated by intoxication
subs: To speak with pleasure, or warmth, or empressement.

def: s.f. A bunch, cluster (of grapes, dates, or plantains, &c.; — syn. ghaud, ghaur).
s.m. A kind of harrow with teeth for eradicating grass from ploughed land.
s.m. Plunging or diving into water, bathing.
adj. (f. -ā), Deep; dense, thick; impervious, impenetrable; inexplicable; obscure; — formidable; great; — s.m. A hiding-place; thicket, wood, forest; a cave; — impenetrable darkness; obscurity

ety: Prk. गहणं or गहनं; S. ग्रहणं, rt. ग्रह्
def: s.m. Seizure of the sun or moon" (by Rāhu), an eclipse
subs: or gahan lagnā, v.n. An eclipse to take place; to be eclipsed.

def: v.n. To be eclipsed (the sun or moon; syn. gahan paṛnā); — to be deformed (the hands or feet).
s.f. Impenetrabil.ty; obscurity.
s.m. = gahanatā, q.v.
adj. Pledged; in pawn; — gahnī dharnā (-ko), To place in pawn.
s.f. The act of caulking; — oakum.
s.f. Holding back; procrastination, delay, tardiness, lateness.
s.m. = gaharī or gahrī, q.v.
s.m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts.
adj. & s.m. corr. of gahvar, q.v.
adv. 'One time,' once; at any time; sometime (syn. kabhī)
subs: adv. At times, sometimes, now and then ( = kabhī-kabhī).

def: adv. contrac. of gāhe, q.v.
adj. (Dakh.) = gahrā, q.v.

ety: prob. by redupl. fr. S. ग्रह्
def: v.n. To quiver, to shake; to wave, to undulate.
adj. Unbroken (a horse).
s.f. An aggregate of five; a five.

ety: redupl. past conj. part. of gāhnā
def: adv. With much inquiry or research.

ety: past conj. part. of gahnā, q.v.
def: adv. Firmly, tightly.
s.m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts.

ety: S. घनाघनं; — for change of n to m; cf. ghūm˚, fr. S. ghūrn
def: adj. Compact, thick, close, dense; crowded; widely diffused; — s.f. Closeness, denseness, crowdedness; diffusedness, strength (of fragrance, &c.); — commotion, stir, bustle, ado.

ety: gāh˚ = Prk. गंह(इ) = S. गृह्ना (ति), rt. ग्रह्
def: v.t. To caulk, thresh, tread out (corn); — to catch, take, seize, stop; — to search, seek, inquire into, investigate.

ety: gah˚ = Prk. गंह(इ), or गेंह(इ) = S. गृहा(ति), rt. ग्रह्
def: v.t. To seize, catch, lay hold of; to take; to handle; to put on; — v.n. To be eclipsed (the sun or moon); — to blush.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi گہنا (ghna /⁠gahnā⁠/) (first attested in c. 1697),, from Prakrit 𑀕𑀳𑀦 (gahana) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit ग्र॑हण (gràhaṇa, “holding, putting on”). Compare later Sanskrit गहना (gahanā, “ornament”), a borrowing from Middle Indo-Aryan.

def: s.m. An ornament, a trinket, a jewel; jewels, jewellery, trinkets; — a pledge, anything in pledge, pawn; mortgage; — a heavy plank (or flat piece of wood) on which a man stands while it is dragged by oxen over a ploughed field to level the ground (syn. paṭṛā); a kind of harrow without teeth
subs: (-ko), To place or put in pawn; to pawn, pledge.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi گہنا (ghna /⁠gahnā⁠/) (first attested in c. 1697),, from Prakrit 𑀕𑀳𑀦 (gahana) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit ग्र॑हण (gràhaṇa, “holding, putting on”). Compare later Sanskrit गहना (gahanā, “ornament”), a borrowing from Middle Indo-Aryan.

def: s.f. A deformed cow born during an eclipse of the moon; — a small (or undersized) cow.
s.m. A large bundle of sheaves (containing about ten seers of grain); — the daily wages in kind of a labourer in harvest-time (also written gairā); a sheaf; a bundle (of grass); a handful.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀕𑀳𑀻𑀭 (gahīra), from Sanskrit गभीर (gabhīrá).

ety: Prk. गहिरओ or गहिलओ; S. गभीर+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Deep, profound; important, weighty, grave; difficult; — deep-seated; — sound (as sleep); — close, intimate (friendship); — strong (as liquor)
subs: s.f. A deep matter; a profound, grave, or important affair, &c.; a difficult matter
(-se), To be close friends (with); — to have a great fight (with)
v.n. To prosper, thrive; to fatten; to gain.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀕𑀳𑀻𑀭 (gahīra), from Sanskrit गभीर (gabhīrá).
